{{DISPLAYTITLE: Object 63: Mimic Gloves}}
'''Object 63:'''
''"Mimic Gloves"''
==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Mimic glove.png|caption=A mimic glove found on the floors of [[Level 3.53]]}}Mimic Gloves are leather work gloves lined with a mechanical exoskeleton, made of an unknown metallic alloy that exhibits great resistance to physical and chemical degradation. The metal components have been recorded to withstand many forms of corrosion, high temperatures, and powerful acids such as Aqua Regia. Despite appearing aged and worn, the exoskeletons show no signs of structural weakness. The exoskeletal design of the gloves is eerily skeletal, featuring thin, overlapping plates that mimic finger bones, a reinforced knuckle guard, and delicate yet unnervingly intricate internal mechanisms. The inner lining is interconnected with a dense array of actuators, pressure-sensitive parts, and micro-mechanical joints reminiscent of high-quality prosthetic hands. These mechanisms are far more intricate and advanced than most other pieces of technology found in [[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]]. The leather exterior of the gloves remains unnaturally pristine, regardless of its surroundings. Dirt, dust, and grime never accumulate on the material, leading researchers to suspect an unknown organic or self-cleaning process. However, no part of the glove appears to be biologically active, nor has it been observed to move on its own without external influence. An unexplainable property of these gloves is the sheer resilience of them. The metal, as mentioned before, is already incredibly durable. However, the durability of the leather should also not go unnoticed, as this leather is incredibly heat resistant, being able to withstand temperatures of around 2,500°C, and is infamously difficult to cut or slice. These properties are entirely unexplainable, as the leather on these glove have been examined to show that they indeed are genetically the exact same as any other sample of buffalo leather obtained in [[The Frontrooms]]. The most prominent and dangerous property of the Mimic Gloves are its ability to amputate and tear hands and flesh. If one were to insert their hand into a Mimic Glove, the exoskeleton of the glove would immediately tighten around the victim's hand. If no measures are taken to remove the glove 2-3 minutes after wearing, the glove will proceed to become impossible to remove, as the leather hardens to a degree in which power tools fail to cut or physically harm the leather. As the glove continues to tighten, circulation is completely cut off from the hand. The affected tissue quickly begins to wither due to oxygen deprivation, and within an hour, necrosis sets in. The result is a full self-amputation, with the hand detaching and falling off, with the amputated hand being entirely decomposed within one to two hours. Researchers have concluded that the gloves "digest" organic material, as flesh and bone break down at an abnormally fast rate compared to natural conditions. The reason for this biological breakdown remains unknown. While the gloves have no apparent need for sustenance, they appear to consume organic matter with no visible purpose. It is not entirely known if these gloves necessarily need to eat flesh. After many experiments by researchers of a joint experiment by The Scrap Barons (more formally known as S.A.R.I.B.), and [[M.E.G.]], it has been concluded that either, the glove enters a state of hibernation, or the glove was never alive. All 3 hypotheses are entirely possible with research taken during this series of experiments. It could be possible that the glove enters a form of "hibernation", as the glove has not been observed to move at all except during its meals, and there is no determining if this glove has the capacity to "die". It may also be plausible that again, the glove was never alive, and its "need" for flesh is simply a coincidence, being that the components within the glove simply have an unexplainable property of breaking down dead material faster than in ideal conditions, and the suffocation mechanics are simply a malfunction. ==Usage== Although these gloves are highly dangerous in their original state, skilled engineers, craftsmen, and mechanics have discovered ways to repurpose them into useful tools. The inner mechanisms of the glove may be tweaked with, although with extreme precaution, often executed by experienced professionals, making these Mimic Gloves into highly functional utility gloves, particularly useful for those working with metal, machinery, and hazardous materials. The leather exterior of the gloves is highly resistant to heat, chemicals, and physical damage, allowing one to handle scalding metal, sharp debris, and corrosive substances with safety. The reinforced exoskeleton enhances grip strength and hand stability, making lifting, welding, and precision-based engineering far more efficient. Due to the internal micro-actuators, a properly modified Mimic Glove can vastly improve the wearer’s strength, allowing them to exert far greater force than a normal human hand could manage. The leading pioneers in the reprogramming and repurposing of these gloves have been the engineers of The Scrap Barons, more formally known as S.A.R.I.B. They were the first documented humans to dismantle and reconstruct Mimic Gloves into useful utility gloves. These gloves also harbor more than just an enhancement, as they may be able to replace a hand once lost. In rare cases, the glove may even give life to those with paralyzed hands. There are also unconfirmed reports of Welded Ones having repurposed and used the gloves as hands for their additional arms. Both repurposed and unmodified Mimic Gloves hold great value among certain groups within the Backrooms. S.A.R.I.B. specifically, considers them a highly sought after salvage, particularly for research and technological advancements, and for uses related to the Blackiron Division's equipment. Other organizations, particularly those dealing in rare or anomalous artifacts, have also shown interest in obtaining them. While individual wanderers rarely seek out these gloves, there are rumors of collectors and rogue engineers willing to trade heavily for them. Due to their rarity and the extreme dangers associated with locations where they can be found, Mimic Gloves, whether modified or in their original state, are considered highly valuable trade commodities. Those who manage to retrieve one may find themselves in possession of a lucrative and useful bargaining tool, provided they know the right people to offer it to. ==Locations== Mimic Gloves are valuable artifacts, being relatively rare to come across, however a prominent pattern is that they seem to be found in industrial levels. They have been confirmed to be all across [[Level 3.53]]. They are most commonly found in piles of scraps and in ravines, however they have been documented to have been found grabbing onto pipes, valves, and lights. There are unconfirmed sightings of Mimic Gloves being found in [[Level 2]] and [[Level 3]], although these claims have no photo evidence to back them up. They have also been found squirming along in the chains in [[Level 192]], although there is no photo evidence, simply being the findings of a trusted [[M.E.G.]] researcher. While Mimic Gloves can only naturally be found in industrial and/or ironworking-heavy levels, their repurposed, safe version can be obtained by countless S.A.R.I.B. merchants across [[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]]. Although they can be found anywhere, S.A.R.I.B. merchants are more often found traveling around [[The Hub]]. [[Category:Objects]] [[Category:Dangerous]]
